candidate_summary: Phase-locked two-agent rhythmic edit correction: pounder emits fixed-cadence token blocks; moderator inserts edits within Δt/2 window; silent overrun drop; N-pounder counterpoint extension

results.title: Pounded yam — Wikipedia
results.func_justification: Source-domain article about yam pounding cuisine; describes the physical fufu preparation, not an LLM/multi-agent edit-correction mechanism. Functional analogue absent.
results.sem_justification: Shares source-domain vocabulary (yam, pounding, mortar, pestle) but no overlap with phase-locked decoding / streaming correction concepts.
results.kw_justification: Wikipedia page about pounded yam covers the source domain (yam pounding fufu). Does not mention pounder-moderator pair as a coordination pair, counterpoint offset, or any LLM-side terms.

results.title: Fufu — Cultures of West Africa
results.func_justification: Cultural/culinary description of fufu preparation; not an LLM or computational coordination scheme.
results.sem_justification: Source-domain only overlap (fufu, pounder, turner). No semantic overlap with LLM streaming or decoding.
results.kw_justification: Mentions fufu preparation involving a pounder and a turner/moderator coordinated rhythm. Two source-domain content words match.

results.title: Iyan — Pounded Yam (My Guide Nigeria)
results.func_justification: Travel/food article on Yoruba iyan; no LLM or computational mechanism.
results.sem_justification: Source-domain only; no overlap with phase-locked decoding.
results.kw_justification: Pounded yam description references source domain only; does not specifically encode pounder-moderator pair coordination as a distinct concept.

results.title: Monitoring Decoding: Mitigating Hallucination via Partial Response Factuality (2025/3)
results.func_justification: Monitoring Decoding introduces a runtime monitor that revises crucial tokens during generation — operationally equivalent to a moderator inserting in-stream edits at the generator's pace. Lacks explicit fixed-cadence Δt/2 window and silent-overrun-drop discipline, but the core two-role in-process edit-correction is present.
results.sem_justification: Strong overlap on dynamic monitor + token revision during decoding; weaker on phase-locked / fixed-cadence framing.
results.kw_justification: Uses 'monitoring decoding' / 'partial response factuality' terminology, none of the candidate's specific content phrases.

results.title: Real-Time Detection of Hallucinated Entities (2025/9)
results.func_justification: Real-time hallucination detection during long-form generation establishes the moderator role: a concurrent detector operating alongside the generator. Functionally captures the moderator-during-stream role though it focuses on detection rather than the fixed-window edit insertion.
results.sem_justification: Significant overlap on real-time streaming + detection-during-generation; missing phase-lock and edit-insertion specifics.
results.kw_justification: No verbatim matches of candidate content-word phrases.

results.title: Detecting & Correcting Hallucinations in LLM-Generated Code (2026/1)
results.func_justification: Concurrent analyzer applies high-confidence corrections during code generation — the two-role rhythmic pair structure. Lacks explicit Δt-fixed-window and silent-overrun-drop but achieves the same effect of in-stream concurrent correction.
results.sem_justification: Strong on real-time concurrent correction during generation; lacks rhythm/phase vocabulary.
results.kw_justification: No matches with candidate content phrases.

results.title: DeCo: Dynamic Correction Decoding (ICLR 2025)
results.func_justification: DeCo is intra-model layer-signal-based correction — single-agent / single-pass mechanism, not a two-agent rhythmic pair. Lacks the moderator role and the phase-locking property.
results.sem_justification: Some overlap on decoding-time correction concept; lacks two-agent / phase / cadence framing.

results.title: Latent Collaboration in MAS (2025/11)
results.func_justification: Structured MAS communication with emergent specialization; not specifically fixed-rhythm phase-locked edit correction. Different mechanism (latent collaboration).
results.sem_justification: General MAS overlap; no rhythmic/phase-locked overlap.
results.kw_justification: No matches with candidate content words.

hit_breakdown.rank_2: kw>=2 (yam pounding fufu, pounder moderator pair)

summary.rationale: total_hits=4 >= 1 under the frozen OR rule. The candidate's LLM application overlaps functionally with three existing concurrent in-process correction/monitoring works (Monitoring Decoding, Real-Time Hallucinated Entity Detection, Hallucination Detection-and-Correction in Code), and a culinary source-domain page also matches 2 source-side content words. The phase-locked / fixed-window / silent-overrun-drop discipline is a distinguishing nuance, but functional equivalence at >=0.7 is reached in multiple effect clusters.
